updates.title: Anthropic Launches Enterprise-Managed Authorization for Claude MCP Connectors (Okta Integration)
updates.summary: Anthropic launched Enterprise-Managed Authorization (EMA), a zero-touch OAuth system allowing enterprise IT admins to provision MCP connector access once via their identity provider (Okta), so employees automatically inherit access on first login with no individual consent screens required. Seven MCP connectors support EMA at launch — Asana, Atlassian, Canva, Figma, Granola, Linear, and Supabase — with Slack coming soon. The feature is live across Claude chat, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork for Team and Enterprise plans, built on the IETF-adopted MCP authorization spec declared stable on June 18, 2026.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Anthropic blog post (claude.com/blog/enterprise-managed-auth), Okta official press release, and multiple trade sources (TechTimes June 19, testingcatalog.com, aiweekly.co). Launch date June 18, 2026 is consistent across sources. MCP spec adoption by IETF in Sept 2025 and declared stable June 18, 2026 is independently verifiable. No red flags.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.183 — Auto Mode Safety Guardrails, Model Deprecation Warnings, and Major Bug Fixes
updates.summary: Claude Code v2.1.183 introduced significant safety guardrails in auto mode: destructive git commands (git reset --hard, git checkout -- ., git clean -fd, git stash drop) are now blocked unless the user explicitly asked to discard local work, and git commit --amend is blocked for commits not made by the agent. Infrastructure destroy commands (terraform destroy, pulumi destroy, cdk destroy) are also blocked unless the specific stack was requested. The release also adds model deprecation warnings, a new attribution.sessionUrl setting, /config --help, and fixes over a dozen bugs spanning subagents, WebSearch, TUI rendering, tmux, and background task handling.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed directly from GitHub releases page (github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases) with exact timestamp June 19, 01:20 UTC. All specific command names and behaviors are from the official changelog. The bug fixes list is exhaustive and matches the code.claude.com changelog. No accuracy concerns.

Slide 2: Commands Now BLOCKED in Auto Mode (git reset --hard, git clean, etc.)
Slide 3: Infrastructure Destroy Commands — Blocked Unless You Asked
Slide 4: New: Model Deprecation Warnings So You're Never Caught Off Guard

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.185 — Stream-Stall Hint UX Improvement
updates.summary: Claude Code v2.1.185 refined the stream-stall notification text from the alarming 'No response from API · Retrying in …' to the calmer 'Waiting for API response · will retry in …', and extended the trigger threshold from 10 seconds of silence to 20 seconds. This is a minor UX polish update that reduces false-alarm anxiety for developers during normal API latency spikes.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed from GitHub releases page with exact timestamp June 20, 20:59 UTC and code.claude.com changelog. Straightforward UX copy change — no verification concerns.
